title: Carduncellus × doumerguei
intro: Carduncellus × doumerguei, also known as Doumergue's cardoon or Artichoke thistle, is a hybrid plant species within the family Asteraceae, derived from the cross between Cynara cardunculus and Cynara scolymus. The plant has been traditionally used for its health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and lowering cholesterol levels, due to its ability to stimulate bile production and enhance gut motility. The bioactive constituents of Carduncellus × doumerguei include cynarin, a lactone that contributes to its medicinal properties, as well as other sesquiterpene lactones and flavonoids. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant are tea infusions, tinctures, and capsules, which are used to support liver and digestive health, as well as to promote weight loss and reduce symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome.
taxonomy: Carduncellus × doumerguei is a hybrid species of thistle and its classification is as follows: it is a member of the family Asteraceae and the genus Carduncellus, and is also known by its synonym Cirsium doumerguei. This species is native to the Mediterranean region. The classification of Carduncellus × doumerguei into its respective categories is as follows: it belongs to the kingdom Plantae, the phylum Magnoliophyta, the class Magnoliopsida, the order Asterales, the family Asteraceae, the genus Carduncellus, and the species C. × doumerguei. It is a thistle species that is often found in dry, rocky areas. The classification of Carduncellus × doumerguei is a combination of its botanical and scientific categorization.
